item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni reach settlement in "It Ends With Us" lawsuit
item.description: A trial in the lawsuit between Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni was set to begin later in May.

item.title: Elon Musk emailed OpenAI two days before trial asking about settlement, court filing show
item.description: The second week of testimony is underway in Elon Musk's lawsuit against Sam Altman's OpenAI. On Monday, an email sent by Musk two days before the trial came under scrutiny. New York Times tech reporter Mike Isaac joins CBS News with more.

item.title: All the past Met Gala themes over the years
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala dress code is "Fashion is Art" — styled to go along with the spring exhibition theme "Costume Art."

item.title: Which states could try to redistrict and add more GOP seats?
item.description: The Supreme Court's ruling on Wednesday about Louisiana's congressional map could have implications for several states, as it narrowed the section of the Voting Rights Act about majority-minority districts.

item.title: Louisiana suspends House primaries for May 16 elections
item.description: Louisiana Secretary of State Nancy Landry said Thursday that the state will suspend its May 16 House primaries in the wake of the Supreme Court striking down​ the state's Congressional map.

item.title: California governor primary is still wide open, CBS News poll finds
item.description: A CBS News poll finds a wide-open contest as voters weigh in on what they want in the state's next governor. CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto breaks down the findings.

item.title: House GOP touts record fundraising haul in first three months of 2026
item.description: The National Republican Congressional Committee, House Republicans' campaign arm, is touting a record-breaking fundraising haul to start the 2026 midterm cycle, the committee chairman told CBS News.

item.title: Billionaires, dark money fuel questions ahead of 2026 midterms
item.description: The ultra-wealthy donor class is getting ready to pour tens of millions into the fall elections that will decide which party will control Congress.

item.title: First glimpse of 2028 Democratic field at National Action Network Convention
item.description: A horde of 2028 Democratic presidential hopefuls attended Al Sharpton's National Action Network conference, nearly two years before the first primary votes will be cast.

item.title: Republican Clay Fuller to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene in House
item.description: Republican Clay Fuller faced Democrat Shawn Harris in a Georgia runoff election after Marjorie Taylor Greene stepped down from her House seat.

item.title: Trump advisers fear GOP midterm losses as gas prices spike
item.description: White House officials have been trying to drum up new strategies to prevent a GOP wipeout in the midterm elections.

item.title: Minnesota elections office subpoenaed in probe over state voter rolls, sources say
item.description: The Minnesota Secretary of State's Office has been ordered to turn over certain voter records.

item.title: Facing potential Latino voter slide, GOP mobilizes in crucial Senate races ahead of 2026 midterms
item.description: Amid signs that Republicans may lose some of the Latino support that the party picked up in 2024, grassroots organizations are stepping in to boost GOP Senate candidates in key midterm races.

item.title: Illinois Senate primary race is test of Pritzker's sway, Democrats' views of ICE
item.description: The 2026 Democratic primary campaign in Illinois for retiring Sen. Dick Durbin's Senate seat — won by Lt. Gov. Lisa Stratton — could set the tone for other midterm primaries.

item.title: What is the SAVE America Act? What to know about the bill Trump is pushing
item.description: President Trump and his allies are pushing Senate Republicans to pass an elections-related bill known as the SAVE America Act.

item.title: Latino Texans fueled Democratic primary turnout. What does it mean for midterms?
item.description: Latino voters helped propel record turnout in last week's Texas Democratic primary, a trend Democrats are watching closely as they try to reclaim ground with the group and pull off an upset win in the red-leaning state's Senate contest.

item.title: Obama calls on voters to help Democrats' Virginia redistricting ahead of midterms
item.description: A special election is being held on April 21 on whether to amend Virginia's constitution to enable redistricting that could help Democrats in the midterm elections.

item.title: Democratic Reps. Green, Menefee advance to runoff in redrawn Texas district
item.description: Rep. Christian Menefee, who was first elected earlier this year in a special election, is running against longtime Rep. Al Green, who had been gerrymandered into the same district.

item.title: James Talarico projected to win Texas Democratic Senate primary
item.description: Texas Rep. James Talarico will win the Democratic Senate primary in Texas, CBS News projects, defeating Rep. Jasmine Crockett in what could be one of the most closely watched races in this year's midterms.

item.title: Trump vows to require voter ID in midterms "whether approved by Congress or not"
item.description: He said there should also be proof of citizenship and a ban on mail-in ballots, with some exceptions.

item.title: House approves the SAVE America Act as GOP makes election bill push
item.description: The SAVE America Act would implement strict new requirements for registering to vote and casting ballots.

item.title: Trump suggests Republicans should nationalize voting
item.description: President Trump has continued to claim without evidence that there is widespread fraud in U.S. elections.

item.title: Democrat Christian Menefee wins special election for U.S. House in Texas
item.description: Democrat Christian Menefee won a Texas U.S. House seat in a special election Saturday that will narrow Republicans' already-slim majority.

item.title: Ahead of midterms, Democrats mobilize around immigration, anti-ICE sentiment
item.description: Immigration may have been a political liability for Democrats​ in 2024. But two years later, it's shaping up to be a top issue they're relying on to galvanize support and mobilize their base.

item.title: Trump visits Iowa to kick off midterm campaigning: "We've got to win"
item.description: Sources say Iowa is seen as a key part of the Trump team's strategy to keep the House in GOP hands.

item.title: RNC clears the way for "midterm convention" later this year
item.description: The Republican National Committee on Friday voted to amend its bylaws to clear the way for a GOP "midterm convention" ahead of November's elections.

item.title: Montana smokejumper aims to join 2026 blue wave Democrats are hoping for
item.description: Democrats are trying to win back the House from the GOP after losing control in the 2022 midterms.

item.title: DNC chair points to 2025 wins as a map out of the wilderness heading into midterms
item.description: "I won't feel like we're out of the wilderness until we win in '28," DNC Chairman Ken Martin told CBS News in the first in a series of interviews with both major parties' leaders ahead of next year's midterms.

item.title: Beshear takes on national role as Dems eye governors to get to the White House
item.description: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear is leading the Democratic Governors Association for the 2026 midterms, with fellow potential presidential candidate Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer handling a vice chair post.

item.title: Latino voters cite affordability, economy as top concerns in new poll
item.description: According to the Unidos Bipartisan Poll of Hispanic Voters: The Road to 2026, 53% of Latino voters cite the cost of living and inflation as their leading concern.
